strings.c(3) Library Functions Manual strings.c(3)

NAME

strings.c - Session string generation and validation implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct word_cache_entry_t
Hashtable entry for cached word lookups.

Macros


#define PCRE2_CODE_UNIT_WIDTH 8
#define SAFE_MALLOC(size, type)
#define SAFE_FREE(ptr)
#define SET_ERRNO(code, fmt, ...)

Functions


void acds_strings_destroy (void)
Cleanup function for session string cache Called by asciichat_shared_destroy() during library cleanup. Safe to call multiple times (idempotent). asciichat_error_t acds_string_init (void)
asciichat_error_t acds_string_generate (char *output, size_t output_size)
bool is_session_string (const char *str)

Detailed Description

Session string generation and validation implementation.

This module generates memorable session strings in the format: adjective-noun-noun (e.g., "swift-river-mountain")

It maintains cached hashtables of adjectives and nouns for fast O(1) validation, ensuring session strings only contain real words.

Definition in file discovery/strings.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define PCRE2_CODE_UNIT_WIDTH 8

#define SAFE_FREE( ptr)

do { if(ptr) { free(ptr); ptr=NULL; } } while(0)

Function
  Documentation




asciichat_error_t
  acds_string_generate (char * output, size_t output_size)


Definition at line 257 of file
  discovery/strings.c.


257                                                                          {
258   if (!output || output_size < SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE) {
259     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "output buffer must be at least %zu bytes",
260                      (size_t)SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE);
261   }
262 
263   // libsodium is guaranteed to be initialized by acds_string_init() before this is called
264   // No need to call sodium_init() again (redundant initialization removed)
265 
266   // Pick random adjective
267   uint32_t adj_idx = simple_randombytes_uniform((uint32_t)adjectives_count);
268   const char *adj = adjectives[adj_idx];
269 
270   // Pick two random nouns
271   uint32_t noun1_idx = simple_randombytes_uniform((uint32_t)nouns_count);
272   uint32_t noun2_idx = simple_randombytes_uniform((uint32_t)nouns_count);
273   const char *noun1 = nouns[noun1_idx];
274   const char *noun2 = nouns[noun2_idx];
275 
276   // Format: adjective-noun-noun
277   int written = safe_snprintf(output, output_size, "%s-%s-%s", adj, noun1, noun2);
278   if (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= output_size) {
279     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW, "Session string too long for buffer");
280   }
281 
282   log_dev("Generated session string: %s", output);
283   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
284 }






References adjectives, adjectives_count,
    nouns, nouns_count, safe_snprintf(),
    SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by database_session_create(),
    options_preset_unified(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t
  acds_string_init (void )


Definition at line 252 of file
  discovery/strings.c.


252                                          {
253   // No initialization needed - getrandom() works directly
254   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
255 }






Referenced by acds_main().






void
  acds_strings_destroy (void )


Cleanup function for session string cache Called by
    asciichat_shared_destroy() during library cleanup. Safe to call
    multiple times (idempotent).


Definition at line 71 of file
  discovery/strings.c.


71                                 {
72   // Shutdown if initialized
73   if (!lifecycle_shutdown(&g_cache_lc)) {
74     return; // Not initialized
75   }
76 
77   // Cleanup adjectives cache
78   word_cache_entry_t *adj_entry, *adj_tmp;
79   HASH_ITER(hh, g_adjectives_cache, adj_entry, adj_tmp) {
80     HASH_DEL(g_adjectives_cache, adj_entry);
81     SAFE_FREE(adj_entry->word);
82     SAFE_FREE(adj_entry);
83   }
84   g_adjectives_cache = NULL;
85 
86   // Cleanup nouns cache
87   word_cache_entry_t *noun_entry, *noun_tmp;
88   HASH_ITER(hh, g_nouns_cache, noun_entry, noun_tmp) {
89     HASH_DEL(g_nouns_cache, noun_entry);
90     SAFE_FREE(noun_entry->word);
91     SAFE_FREE(noun_entry);
92   }
93   g_nouns_cache = NULL;
94 
95   log_dev("Session string word cache cleaned up");
96 }






References lifecycle_shutdown(), SAFE_FREE, and
    word_cache_entry_t::word.


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().






bool is_session_string (const char * str)


Definition at line 286 of file
  discovery/strings.c.


286                                         {
287   if (!str || str[0] == '\0') {
288     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string is NULL or empty");
289     return false;
290   }
291 
292   size_t len = strlen(str);
293 
294   // Check length bounds (min 4, max defined by SESSION_STRING_MAX_LEN)
295   if (len < 4 || len > SESSION_STRING_MAX_LEN) {
296     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string length %zu outside valid range 4-%d", len, SESSION_STRING_MAX_LEN);
297     return false;
298   }
299 
300   // Session strings must be ASCII-only (homograph attack prevention)
301   if (!utf8_is_ascii_only(str)) {
302     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string contains non-ASCII characters");
303     return false;
304   }
305 
306   // Get compiled regex (lazy initialization)
307   pcre2_code *regex = session_format_regex_get();
308   if (!regex) {
309     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Session string PCRE2 regex failed to initialize");
310     return false;
311   }
312 
313   // Validate format using PCRE2 regex
314   pcre2_match_data *match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(regex, NULL);
315   if (!match_data) {
316     log_error("Failed to allocate match data for session string regex");
317     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate match data");
318     return false;
319   }
320 
321   int match_result = pcre2_jit_match(regex, (PCRE2_SPTR8)str, len, 0, 0, match_data, NULL);
322 
323   pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
324 
325   if (match_result < 0) {
326     // Format validation failed
327     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string format does not match pattern");
328     return false;
329   }
330 
331   // Extract the three words by finding hyphens
332   // Format is guaranteed to be: word-word-word where each word is 2-12 lowercase letters
333   const char *hyphen1 = strchr(str, '-');
334   if (!hyphen1) {
335     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string missing first hyphen");
336     return false;
337   }
338 
339   const char *hyphen2 = strchr(hyphen1 + 1, '-');
340   if (!hyphen2) {
341     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string missing second hyphen");
342     return false;
343   }
344 
345   // Extract three words
346   size_t adj_len = hyphen1 - str;
347   size_t noun1_len = hyphen2 - hyphen1 - 1;
348   size_t noun2_len = len - (hyphen2 - str) - 1;
349 
350   if (adj_len >= 32 || noun1_len >= 32 || noun2_len >= 32) {
351     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string word length out of bounds");
352     return false;
353   }
354 
355   char adj[32], noun1[32], noun2[32];
356   memcpy(adj, str, adj_len);
357   adj[adj_len] = '\0';
358   memcpy(noun1, hyphen1 + 1, noun1_len);
359   noun1[noun1_len] = '\0';
360   memcpy(noun2, hyphen2 + 1, noun2_len);
361   noun2[noun2_len] = '\0';
362 
363   // Lazy initialization: build validation caches on first use
364   // call_once ensures thread-safe exactly-once execution
365   build_validation_caches();
366   // If initialization fails silently (logs warning), hashtables will be empty
367   // and validation will fail, which is safe behavior
368 
369   // Validate first word is an adjective
370   word_cache_entry_t *adj_entry = NULL;
371   HASH_FIND_STR(g_adjectives_cache, adj, adj_entry);
372   if (!adj_entry) {
373     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string first word '%s' is not a valid adjective", adj);
374     return false;
375   }
376 
377   // Validate second and third words are nouns
378   word_cache_entry_t *noun_entry1 = NULL;
379   HASH_FIND_STR(g_nouns_cache, noun1, noun_entry1);
380   if (!noun_entry1) {
381     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string second word '%s' is not a valid noun", noun1);
382     return false;
383   }
384 
385   word_cache_entry_t *noun_entry2 = NULL;
386   HASH_FIND_STR(g_nouns_cache, noun2, noun_entry2);
387   if (!noun_entry2) {
388     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string third word '%s' is not a valid noun", noun2);
389     return false;
390   }
391 
392   log_dev("Valid session string: %s", str);
393   return true;
394 }






References SET_ERRNO, and utf8_is_ascii_only().


Referenced by database_session_create(),
    discover_session_parallel(), and parse_client_address().

Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>
